layer3_inference: MWA is likely engaged in emergency procurement or has contacted neighboring utilities about emergency interconnects/mutual aid
rejection_reason: FLAGGED classification (2 assumptions) combined with zero data support. No evidence of emergency procurement, mutual aid activation, or alternative recovery actions exists in any available data source. The inference is based on what a rational operator WOULD do, which per [LZ-CC-001] is a logically coherent narrative without verifiable real-world anchoring. Plausibility is not evidence.

layer3_inference: MWA may have deliberately suppressed or overridden automatic shutdown protections on Pump 3
rejection_reason: Not fully rejected but reduced to 75% discount. Two assumptions (system has auto-shutdown capability; operator overrode it). Only partial data support (one data point: alarm triggered but pump not shut down). We don't know this system's protection design. The observation (alarm + continued operation) could equally indicate no auto-shutdown exists. Per [LZ-RC-004], the simpler explanation (system lacks auto-shutdown for this condition) must be tested before the more complex one (operator override). Retained as context only at minimal weight.
